sources_exist.note: All seven canonical URL occurrences resolve to accessible pages.

figures_match_sources.note: The current-adoption and owner-age figures match their sources, and the pricing page supports line-level commission ranges. However, the historical-analog page does not support a 10-12 year path to 50%, the exit page reports 19.4x for delegated-authority firms rather than 13.0x for retail brokerage platforms, the active-consolidator article does not establish 40 PE-backed platforms, and the buy/pricing pages do not establish the exact median and revenue-mix inputs used.

judgment_inputs_plausible.note: The bounded fractions for identified processing roles are plausible, as are the PRESSURED terminal class and moderate moat. But the unlisted 31.94% wage tail is asserted to be predominantly back-office and assigned 55% automation without occupational evidence; that assumption supplies 0.17567 of the 0.46856 total and pushes V to its cap. t50 and pi_dist also rest on unsupported analog and billing-share evidence, so the score-driving judgments are not adequately grounded.

rubric_consistent.note: All four deterministic dataset objects match the supplied dataset exactly, formulas follow the frozen rubric, the heterogeneous dominant segment is identified, and PE evidence is confined to B and M. ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, M, S is explicitly reviewed; the nonzero differences are within the orchestrator tolerance and are not treated as a rubric failure.

cross_checks_honest.note: All mandatory cross-checks are present and candid about direct-channel disruption, commercial complexity protection, carrier commission compression, and the distinction between operator cost reduction and product replacement. The narratives are internally honest, though their quantitative evidence is insufficient for acceptance.

source_audits.note: Insurance Journal reports 695 agency deals in 2025, says PE-backed/hybrid buyers continued to dominate, and names BroadStreet, Hub, Inszone, World, and Keystone among leaders. The accessible article does not report 96 unique buyers, a 73% PE-backed deal share, or a count of 40 active PE-backed platforms.

The page reports 7.0x-9.0x adjusted EBITDA for $2M-$10M-revenue LMM brokerages and separately defines owner-compensation normalization. It does not establish 7.0x as the weighted median for the record's $1M-$10M EBITDA band; 7.0x is the bottom of the cited range, while the record itself calls 8.0x the midpoint.

The Big I ACT article directly reports 8% with AI embedded in daily workflows, 22% using AI in limited areas, 33% experimenting, and 31% not currently using AI. This supports the 8% material-adoption input and its scope.

MarshBerry's page is explicitly about delegated-authority firms such as MGUs, MGAs, program administrators, and coverholders and reports a 19.4x average pro forma EBITDA multiple for 2025 transactions. It does not report 11.8x for $1M+ EBITDA retail brokers, a 12.0x-16.0x primary-platform range, or a 13.0x retail-agency exit input.

IA Magazine reports e-signature usage rising from 61% in 2022 to 70% in 2024. The page does not show when adoption began, a 10-12 year time to 50%, or 90%+ agency-management-system adoption, so it cannot support the declared historical analog.
The article directly states that 40% of surveyed agency principals were older than 60 and 20% were older than 65, supporting the owner-age value and target population.

The vendor page explains that carriers pay embedded commissions and reports P&C line-level ranges, including personal-property and commercial rates, plus 2%-5% of premium for contingent commissions. It does not establish that 85%-90% of agency revenue is commission or that fee-based large-commercial work is 10%-15% of total revenue; its separate 64% high-performing-agency statistic concerns non-commission revenue exceeding 15%, not an industrywide revenue mix.

reasons: inputs.exit_mult.url (https://www.marshberry.com/resource/to-the-point/2025-2026-valuation-update-firms-with-delegated-authority-reach-all-time-highs/) reports a 19.4x average for delegated-authority MGA/MGU-type firms, not a 13.0x exit benchmark for the dominant retail independent-agency segment. Re-run with retail brokerage platform transactions or credible comps matching the $10M+ EBITDA exit tier and earnings basis.

inputs.buy_mult.url (https://ctacquisitions.com/guides/insurance-agency-ma-multiples-2026/) supports a 7.0x-9.0x LMM range but not 7.0x as the weighted median for $1M-$10M EBITDA targets. Re-run with size-matched transaction/cohort evidence establishing the median used, including owner-compensation and contingent-commission normalization.

inputs.historical_analogs.url (https://www.iamagazine.com/news/7-findings-from-the-2024-agency-universe-study/) provides only a 2022-to-2024 e-signature change and no 10-12 year adoption path or AMS series. Re-run with a dated comparable-technology series that directly establishes time to roughly 50% and use it in the t50_years derivation.

inputs.active_consolidators.url (https://www.insurancejournal.com/news/national/2026/01/22/855124.htm) does not establish 40 active PE-backed consolidators. Re-run with a named-platform inventory or tracker whose inclusion window and PE-backing criterion directly support the count used in B.

inputs.pricing_structure.url (https://www.sonant.ai/blog/insurance-agent-commission-structure) does not substantiate the 85%-90% commission-revenue and 10%-15% fee-revenue mix used in pi_dist and the pricing cross-check. Re-run with representative agency financial benchmarking that reports revenue shares by billing model.

inputs.ai_replaceable_share.breakdown_by_role[10] assigns a 0.55 automatable fraction to the unlisted 31.94% wage tail by asserting it is predominantly accounting, compliance, IT, HR, and admin, without evidence for that composition. This single assumption contributes 0.17567 and drives V to 10. Re-run with an evidenced decomposition of the residual occupation share and role-specific fractions, including any producer, management, specialist, or relationship-heavy roles in the tail.
flags_reviewed: ARITH_DELTA_WITHIN_TOL: nonzero stored-vs-recomputed deltas on A, B, M, S
